name: Oysters Rockefeller (Gourmet Mag.)
recipeInstructions:
1. Shuck oysters and reserve the liquor and shells.
2. Clean and dry the shells.
3. In a bowl, combine lettuce, spinach, scallion, 1/2 cup bread crumbs, parsley, celery, and garlic.
4. In a skillet, melt butter over moderate heat and cook the spinach mixture for 1-2 minutes or until wilted.
5. Stir in Pernod, anchovy paste, cayenne, and salt and pepper to taste. Let cool.
6. Chill the mixture, covered, for 1 hour.
7. In another skillet, cook bacon over moderate heat until crisp. Drain on paper towels.
8. Arrange one oyster in each shell and moisten with reserved liquor.
9. Spread half of the spinach mixture onto each oyster using heaping tablespoons.
10. Sprinkle bacon evenly over each oyster.
11. Top each oyster with remaining spinach mixture and sprinkle with 1 Tbsp bread crumbs.
12. Place oysters on oven-proof platters filled with coarse salt.
13. Bake in a preheated 450°F (230°C) oven for 18 minutes or until the bread crumbs are well-browned.
14. Garnish with parsley sprigs and lemon wedges, then serve.
